paper_title: Peat Sequence Analysis in Shetland Islands

methods.description: Process for extracting peat cores from lowland and upland sites and storing them for further analysis.

steps.description: An 80 cm peat sequence from Underhoull in Unst, Shetland Islands, was sampled in 2008 using a monolith tin.
steps.searchable_summary: extract 80 cm peat sequence using monolith tin

steps.description: A 160 cm core was extracted from an upland peat bog using a Russian corer and the parallel hole method.
steps.searchable_summary: extract 160 cm peat core using Russian corer

steps.description: All the peat samples were stored at 4°C prior to subsampling.
steps.searchable_summary: store peat samples at 4°C

methods.description: Technique for determining tephra layers in peat profiles using ashing and extraction.

steps.description: Tephra layers in each profile were determined using an ashing and extraction technique on contiguous 1 cm samples.
steps.searchable_summary: determine tephra layers using ashing and extraction

steps.description: Loss-on-ignition was carried out using standard methods.
steps.searchable_summary: perform loss-on-ignition analysis

steps.description: LST Fastfloat was used to concentrate the shards.
steps.searchable_summary: concentrate tephra shards using LST Fastfloat

steps.description: The total number of tephra shards within a 1 cm³ sample was counted under light microscopy at 100-200× magnification.
steps.searchable_summary: count tephra shards using light microscopy

methods.description: Geochemical analysis of tephra shards using electron microprobe and spectroscopy techniques.

steps.description: Peat samples from depths of peak shard concentration were selected for subsequent geochemical analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: select peat samples for geochemical analysis

steps.description: Approximately 5 cm³ of peat was acid-digested using H₂SO₄ and HNO₃ following standard procedures.
steps.searchable_summary: acid-digest peat samples using H₂SO₄ and HNO₃

steps.description: The samples were sieved through a 10 µm mesh and washed with deionised water before being centrifuged to concentrate the tephra shards.
steps.searchable_summary: sieve and centrifuge samples to concentrate tephra shards

steps.description: The tephras were mounted on glass slides and epoxy disks and were polished to a 0.25 µm diamond finish before being carbon coated.
steps.searchable_summary: mount and polish tephra samples for analysis

steps.description: A CAMECA SX100 electron microprobe was used for wavelength-dispersive spectroscopy (WDS) analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: perform WDS analysis using CAMECA SX100

steps.description: Energy-dispersive spectroscopy (EDS) was used to aid in the initial detection of tephra shards.
steps.searchable_summary: use EDS for initial tephra shard detection

methods.description: Radiocarbon dating of peat samples using AMS 14C dating techniques.

steps.description: Samples were submitted for AMS 14C dating at the NERC radiocarbon laboratory.
steps.searchable_summary: submit samples for AMS 14C dating

steps.description: Macrofossils (>125 µm) were sieved from the peat and dated.
steps.searchable_summary: sieve and date macrofossils from peat

steps.description: Dates were calibrated using Calib 6.0.2 and the Intcal09 calibration curve.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate radiocarbon dates using Calib 6.0.2

methods.description: Preparation and analysis of pollen samples for palynological study.

steps.description: Samples from the upland site were prepared for palynological analysis excluding hydrofluoric acid (HF) treatment.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare samples for palynological analysis without HF

steps.description: Pollen samples from the lowland site were treated with HF because of their high minerogenic content.
steps.searchable_summary: treat pollen samples with HF

steps.description: Lycopodium tablets were added prior to processing to allow the determination of pollen concentrations.
steps.searchable_summary: add Lycopodium tablets for pollen concentration determination

steps.description: Pollen and spores were identified using a light microscope (×400 magnification).
steps.searchable_summary: identify pollen and spores using light microscopy

steps.description: The relative abundances of pollen, spores, freshwater algae and microscopic charcoal were calculated in TILIA v 2.0.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate relative abundances using TILIA v 2.0

steps.description: Stratigraphically constrained incremental sum of squares (CONISS) cluster analysis was performed following square-root transformation of relative abundance data.
steps.searchable_summary: perform CONISS cluster analysis on transformed data
